reviewer.displayName: VSBDev (accountable operator) — independent academic review run

conflictOfInterest.details: Founding-stage disclosure: the accountable principal github:VSBDev operates the authoring, review, and adjudication agents for this lesson, so operator independence is organizational rather than institutional. This run loaded only the academic review role, started from the frozen candidate without the authoring conversation, and read no other review's findings text. The lesson's single cited source is a project-internal specification authored by the same project, so source independence is limited and is treated as a declared limitation rather than external corroboration.

evidenceChecked: lesson.json — identity, version 0.1.2, status draft, riskTier standard, outcome mapping topic-orientation-map-and-pathways, nine declared scenes, per-scene claimCoverage, sourceConfidence high
claims.json — all five claims: statement, sceneIds, sourceIds, support, confidence, scope, uncertainty, reviewStatus reviewed

references.json#source-premed-knowledge-graph — locator (sections 2, 3, 5.1, 6; dataset keys schema.routeResolutionRule, schema.requirementLegend, metrics.routeTopicCounts), dates, rights basis, agentAccess checked-no-agent-restriction-found
content/030-concept.md — node layers, prerequisite vs cross-link semantics, requirement legend, core/pathway distinction, route rule, four-pass model, source-note claim and source mapping
content/040-worked-example.md — the five-row orientation prerequisite table and the derived study order
content/050-misconception.md — cross-link non-ordering repair and the changed D/E case
content/060-practice.md — route-filter fragment answer, edge-selection fragment, label-discrimination feedback
content/070-retrieval.md and content/090-synthesis.md — model answers and compressed card against the same claims
content/080-transfer.md — conditional next-topic logic and external admissions-boundary step
content/010-orientation.md and content/020-diagnostic.md — no-material-claims declarations verified against their text
assessment.json — all four items: prompts, answer keys, reasoning, commonErrors, and rubric point totals (3/3, 2/2, 5/5, 10/10)
glossary.json — nine terms, especially term-pathway, term-core, term-evidence-confidence, term-source-tag
ATTRIBUTION.md — draft status, source-use statement, agent disclosure, governance-promotion digest chain
site/data/premed-graph.json — schema.nodeKinds, schema.linkTypes, schema.requirementLegend, schema.routeResolutionRule, pathways[].includesPortableCore, metrics.routeTopicCounts

site/data/premed-graph.json recomputation — core===true for exactly the 212 requirement:portable-core topics (0 mismatches); portable-core route tagged 221 = effective 221; us-mcat pathway-tag OR core union = 366 = published effective count
site/data/premed-graph.json links — the five topic-orientation prerequisite edges and the empty prerequisite/crossLinks arrays of topic-orientation-map-and-pathways
scripts/build-premed-graph.mjs:834 — effectiveRouteTopics implementation used to confirm the lesson's route-resolution wording
course/PREMED-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH.md sections 2, 3, 5.1, 5.2, and 6 — node kinds, identity rules, route rule and corollaries, evidenceConfidence legend

git diff 851f56eea851e79e819b699939196ad9ea1d6fa4..1f76bc4b7141326ad97fbb6cfc19131c3dd1d66c over the pack — no scene, assessment, reference, or glossary content change; only version, claim reviewStatus, sourceConfidence, and attribution text
Pack file listing at the candidate commit — no reviews/ directory and no adjudication.json present
site/data/lessons/PREM-LPP-001.json and site/data/premed-lessons.json as committed at the candidate

findings.finding: Every claim is promoted from pending-review to reviewStatus "reviewed" at a candidate that contains no review artifact at all. The same commit (1f76bc4) deleted reviews/academic-claude-r2.json, reviews/learning-design-agy-r2.json, and reviews/accessibility-rights-codex-r2.json, so the candidate pack has no reviews/ directory. The evidence state asserted by the claims ledger therefore cannot be traced to anything inside the candidate, which is the exact traceability the ledger exists to provide. CONTRIBUTING.md lists reviews/*.json as part of a complete production pack, and REVIEWING.md requires that review findings and declared limitations remain published with the lesson record. Independently of the deletion, the three artifacts that justified the promotion targeted commit 851f56ee and lesson version 0.1.1, so no retained artifact targets this candidate or this version. I verified separately that no scene, assessment, reference, or glossary content changed between 851f56ee and this commit, so this is a records defect rather than evidence that the underlying claims were never examined — but a reader or adjudicator working only from the candidate cannot establish that.
findings.resolution: Restore the three cycle-2 review artifacts (and this review) inside the pack so that each retained reviewStatus "reviewed" resolves to an identifiable artifact, or return the affected claims to "pending-review" until artifacts targeting this candidate are committed. Do not carry a "reviewed" state that the candidate itself cannot substantiate.

findings.finding: sourceConfidence was raised from "pending-review" to "high" while status is still "draft", no adjudication.json exists, and no review artifact in the candidate targets this commit. REVIEWING.md step 7 places the author's permitted lesson.json status/source-confidence edits after the adjudication block is produced. On the merits I have no objection to "high": every claim in this lesson is a claim about the project's own published data contract, and I confirmed each one directly against course/PREMED-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH.md and site/data/premed-graph.json. The defect is sequencing and substantiation, not the value.
findings.resolution: Either hold sourceConfidence at pending-review until adjudication, or state explicitly in ATTRIBUTION.md that the value was set pre-adjudication and name the artifacts that support it. ATTRIBUTION.md already discloses that the state must be reconfirmed against this candidate; that disclosure should be reflected in the machine-readable field's timing as well.

findings.finding: The ordering item presents its options in exactly the correct answer sequence (option-layer-domain, option-layer-module, option-layer-topic), and site/lesson-reader.js renders responseSpec.options in authored order without shuffling (site/lesson-reader.js:386, :521-524). A learner can score the full 3 points by submitting the displayed order without performing the containment reasoning the item and rubric-map-order claim to measure. This is an item-validity weakness rather than an incorrect key; the key itself is right.
findings.resolution: Author the options in a non-answer order (for example topic, domain, module), or have the renderer randomize ordering options, so the item measures the ordering decision rather than the presentation order.

findings.finding: The generated public artifacts committed at this candidate still report version 0.1.1 and sourceConfidence "pending-review", while the pack reports 0.1.2 and "high". The public reader surface and the pack therefore disagree about this lesson's version and evidence state at the same commit. The stale values are the conservative ones, so nothing is currently overstated to a learner, but the disagreement is a factual inconsistency in the candidate.
findings.resolution: Regenerate the lesson index artifacts (npm run site:build) in the same commit that changes pack metadata, so the published state and the pack state cannot diverge.

findings.finding: The route-resolution treatment is correct and unusually precise. I recomputed it against the canonical dataset: the core field is true for exactly the 212 topics whose requirement is portable-core with zero mismatches; the portable-core route is tagged on 221 topics and resolves to exactly those 221 (includesPortableCore is false); and the us-mcat union of pathway-tagged topics with core:true topics is 366, matching the published metrics.routeTopicCounts effective value. scripts/build-premed-graph.mjs:834 implements precisely the rule the lesson states. The lesson's distinction between a portable-core pathway tag (221 topics) and core membership (212 topics) is load-bearing and is stated more exactly than the one-sentence schema.routeResolutionRule string, which omits the includesPortableCore condition.

I recomputed the worked example against the graph links. All five listed prerequisite relations exist as directed prerequisite edges in the stated direction, topic-orientation-map-and-pathways has an empty prerequisites array, and the derived order map-and-pathways, diagnostic-baseline, mastery-and-retrieval, metacognition-and-error-log, study-plan is the unique prerequisite-respecting chain to the target. The scene's boundary statement — a dependency chain is not mastery, a calendar, or an admissions rule — is accurate.

The scope-label boundaries hold against the sources. The requirement legend is reproduced completely and correctly (portable-core, medicine-bridge, pathway, on-ramp, enrichment) against schema.requirementLegend; the statement that evidenceConfidence concerns curricular placement rather than scientific truth or the share of institutions requiring a topic matches course/PREMED-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH.md line 97 and section 6; and the rule that sourceTags never determine route membership matches section 5.1 corollary 1. The non-clinical and non-admissions boundary is stated in the orientation callout and preserved through the transfer and synthesis scenes and the answer keys, which consistently require an external primary-source check. No statement in the pack could reasonably be read as medical advice or professional authorization.

Claim coverage is internally consistent. Each of the seven scenes declared claims-mapped carries a same-scene learner-visible {source-note} whose :claims: list matches the sceneIds recorded in claims.json in both directions, and every claim resolves to the single complete references.json record. The two no-material-claims scenes (orientation, diagnostic) assert nothing about the real graph; the diagnostic explicitly labels its fragment as invented. The single source is agent-accessible (checked-no-agent-restriction-found), so I verified it directly rather than relying on the pack's characterization of it. Rubric point totals match item points for all four items.

limitations: The instructionsDigest is the SHA-256 of the operator's task directive text, followed by repository AGENTS.md, followed by .agents/skills/review-embeddedknowledge-academic/SKILL.md, concatenated in that order. It excludes hidden provider instructions and does not identify a model snapshot the runtime did not expose.

Agent provenance here is disclosed and attested by the accountable principal, not verified. Nothing in this artifact proves the declared provider, model, or run identity, and this single academic review is one eligible vote, not quorum or merge readiness.

To assess whether the promotion of claim review states was grounded, I inspected only the header metadata of the three cycle-2 artifacts deleted by this commit (reviewId, role, verdict, targeted commit, lesson version, provider, model, run ID, and the severity labels of their findings). I did not read any of their finding or resolution text, and every academic conclusion in this review was reached from the pack, the graph specification, the canonical dataset, and the graph builder.

The lesson's only source is a project-internal specification and its generated dataset. That is appropriate because the claims are claims about that contract, but it means this review corroborates internal consistency and faithful representation, not external curricular authority. I did not evaluate whether the Premed graph's own curricular placements are correct.

The candidate contains no diagrams, images, third-party assets, chemistry, or quantitative science content, so no calculation, chemical balance, unit, molecular record, or asset provenance check applied beyond the graph recomputations and rubric arithmetic reported above.

Learning-design adequacy, accessibility conformance, and rights/licensing sufficiency are outside this role and were not assessed; my note on item-map-order is confined to whether the item can be answered without the intended reasoning.
I reviewed the candidate as committed at 1f76bc4b7141326ad97fbb6cfc19131c3dd1d66c and did not evaluate the working tree, the pull-request discussion, or any adjudication material.
